name: Lysandre Debut
bio: Lysandre Debut is the Chief Open Source Officer at Hugging Face and a core maintainer of the Transformers library, the most widely used framework for state-of-the-art machine learning models in text, vision, audio, and multimodal domains. He co-authored the seminal paper "Transformers: State-of-the-Art Natural Language Processing" presented at EMNLP 2020, and was the first engineer at Hugging Face to focus entirely on the open-source mission. Educated at ESEO Angers in Computer Science Engineering with a Data Science major (2016-2019), he joined Hugging Face in 2019 and has since led the growth of the Transformers library from 40 architectures and 20,000 daily downloads to over 400 architectures and 3 million daily pip installs. In January 2026, he shipped Transformers v5 -- the first major version release in five years -- featuring performance optimizations, a unified tokenizer backend, PyTorch-first focus, and deep ecosystem interoperability.

timeline.event: Co-authored "HuggingFace's Transformers: State-of-the-art Natural Language Processing" (arXiv preprint)
Paper presented at EMNLP 2020 System Demonstrations; Transformers v4.0.0-rc-1 released
Lightning talk at PyTorch Conference 2023: "Diffusers: Bringing Cutting-Edge Diffusion Models to the Masses"
Announced kick-off of Transformers v5 development with ArthurZ and cyrilvallez
Published Transformers v5 blog post and released v5.0.0rc0 -- the first major release in five years
Released Transformers v5.0.0 stable -- 400+ architectures, 3M daily installs, PyTorch-first, unified tokenizers
Released Transformers v5.3.0 (EuroBERT, VibeVoice ASR, TimesFM2.5, and more)
Contributed project governance and security contribution guide to huggingface/safetensors

key_contributions.title: Hugging Face Transformers
key_contributions.description: Core maintainer and co-author of the Transformers library, the de facto standard for state-of-the-art ML models. Over 400 architectures, 750,000+ model checkpoints on the Hub, and 3 million daily pip installs. Led the Transformers v5 release.

key_contributions.title: Transformers v5
key_contributions.description: Led the first major version release in five years. Introduced modular model definitions, unified tokenizer backend (removing slow/fast split), PyTorch-first backend, AttentionInterface abstraction, and deep interoperability with vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp, MLX, and TensorRT.

key_contributions.title: EMNLP 2020 Paper: Transformers Library
key_contributions.description: Co-authored the foundational paper "Transformers: State-of-the-Art Natural Language Processing" (Wolf, Debut et al.), published at EMNLP 2020 and cited thousands of times.

key_contributions.title: Safetensors Adoption
key_contributions.description: Drove the default adoption of safetensors serialization in Transformers (v4.35+), improving model loading safety and performance by replacing pickle-based torch files.

key_contributions.title: Hugging Face Diffusers
key_contributions.description: Contributed to the Diffusers library for state-of-the-art pretrained diffusion models, enabling generation of images, audio, and 3D structures.

key_contributions.title: Benchmarking Transformers
key_contributions.description: Published benchmarking analyses comparing PyTorch and TensorFlow performance for Transformer inference, guiding framework optimization decisions.

sources.title: Transformers v5: Simple model definitions powering the AI ecosystem (HF Blog)
Lysandre's Transformers v5 RC announcement on X
